description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, stone-like material of the raised sections. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, presenting a smooth, dark finish. The block appears robust and heavy, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its design suggests both functional and aesthetic considerations, possibly to prevent passage or to delineate space while allowing water drainage. The overall appearance is modern and utilitarian, suitable for integration into cityscapes or public infrastructure.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a bold striped pattern. The sides are smooth and dark, giving it a modern, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped top surface with parallel grooves and a solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes appearing more pronounced.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped top and a solid, dark rear face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the deep grooves and the alternating light and dark pattern.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
